title: Review: nie-22n wallet send_payment
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — One real correctness bug (Sent notification lost on channel close/MLS failure with no recovery path), one efficiency bug (51.5MB blocking sync I/O per payment on the async runtime), and two schema-level land mines (no SQL account filter, no index on spent). None are immediate user-visible failures at current scale (no real scanner, no real LWD), but nie-ugn.1 will generate support issues the moment the first real payment is attempted and the connection drops at the wrong moment.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: did not execute any real payment end-to-end (no live LWD, no real Sapling params, no funded wallet). The Sapling proof-generation path (build_shielded_tx → zcash_primitives builder) was read but not exercised. Would raise confidence if: integration test with real params files and a real testnet node. The fee consistency argument (Item 1 in correctness review) relies on the ZIP-317 grace floor absorbing a 1-action discrepancy when n=1; this is correct but not tested.
